status.comment: Hardkernel ramped up production ~30% in early 2025 after demand outstripped supply; both 2 GB and 4 GB SKUs are still actively sold (checked 2026-04-26).

open-source-hardware.comment: Hardkernel publishes schematics PDFs but does not release PCB design files under an OSHW-compatible license.

country-of-origin.comment: Hardkernel Co., Ltd. is headquartered in AnYang, South Korea.

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: Hardkernel quotes 2.016 GHz sustained without throttling under the bundled heatsink.

opengl-es-version.comment: Mali-G31 hardware advertises OpenGL ES 3.2; vendor blob driver supports up to 3.2, while open-source Panfrost driver currently caps at 3.1 on Bifrost.

vulkan-support.comment: No production Vulkan driver for Mali-G31 on Linux as of 2026-04; Panfrost Vulkan effort has not landed for Bifrost-class GPUs.

npu.comment: Amlogic S905X3 has no dedicated NPU.

hw-video-decode.comment: S905X3 hardware decodes 4K H.265 (10-bit, up to 75 fps), H.264, and VP9; AV1 is not supported.

ram-min-gb.comment: 2 GB SKU sold separately at $50 alongside the 4 GB model.

emmc.comment: Onboard eMMC 5.x module socket; Hardkernel sells 8/16/32/64 GB modules.

sata.comment: C4 has no native SATA. The sister board ODROID-HC4 adds SATA via a JMicron USB-to-SATA bridge — still not native.

wifi.comment: No onboard Wi-Fi; vendor sells optional USB Wi-Fi modules.

bluetooth.comment: No onboard Bluetooth; requires a USB dongle.

usb-a-3x-ports.comment: Four USB 3.0 host ports behind a single root hub.

usb-a-2-ports.comment: Only USB 2.0 connection is the OTG micro-USB port (not USB-A).

usb-c-ports-data.comment: No USB-C; the USB 2.0 OTG port uses micro-USB.

poe-support.comment: RJ45 magjack does not pass PoE; no Hardkernel PoE HAT for the C4.

mipi-dsi.comment: No MIPI-DSI connector; HDMI is the only display output.

mipi-csi.comment: No MIPI-CSI camera connector exposed; cameras are USB-only on the C4.

audio-jack.comment: No 3.5 mm jack on board. Only audio outputs are HDMI plus a 7-pin header that can drive S/PDIF or an external I2S DAC.

gpio-pins.comment: 40-pin J2 header; 25 user-accessible GPIOs plus power, I²C, SPI, UART, PWM, and 2× 12-bit ADC.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: 40-pin layout follows the RPi GPIO arrangement at 3.3 V; some HATs require board-specific drivers.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Mechanical: 85×56 mm board with RPi-compatible mounting holes lets HATs bolt on. Electrical: no HAT EEPROM auto-config — pinout matches RPi but software setup is per-HAT.

idle-power-w.comment: Hardkernel-published measurement; CNX-Software reproduces the same number in head-to-head with RPi 4.

load-power-w.comment: Hardkernel cites 3.1–3.3 W under heavy load with bundled heatsink; CNX measured 3.11 W under CPU stress.

cooling-required.comment: Hardkernel ships and recommends the bundled aluminum heatsink for sustained 2 GHz operation; no fan needed.

rtc-battery-header.comment: Onboard RTC circuit with a 2-pin header for the optional Hardkernel backup battery.

form-factor-class.comment: 85×56 mm matches RPi-standard footprint; mounting holes follow the RPi pattern.

weight-g.comment: Hardkernel quotes 59 g including the bundled heatsink; bare-board weight is lower but not separately published.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Mainline U-Boot has a dedicated odroid-c4 defconfig; Armbian ships current 6.18.x mainline kernel images with Ethernet/USB/HDMI/GPIO working. GPU acceleration via Panfrost requires extra patches and currently caps at OpenGL ES 3.1.

official-os-image.comment: Hardkernel maintains Ubuntu 22.04/24.04 and legacy Android images for the C4.

distros-with-images.comment: Vendor Ubuntu image plus community Armbian (Ubuntu Noble/Resolute, Debian Trixie/Forky/Sid) and DietPi support. CoreELEC/LibreELEC also ship images, but those are media-center distros not in the defined tag set.

bootloader.comment: Mainline U-Boot supported via odroid-c4 defconfig; Hardkernel also ships a Petitboot loader on SPI flash that can be wiped to use clean mainline.

long-term-support.comment: Hardkernel does not publish a formal support window. Empirically, the C4 has had vendor and community kernel updates for 5+ years since 2020 launch and remains actively supported on Armbian's current branch in 2026.

android-support.comment: Hardkernel released an Android 9 Pie image; no newer Android version maintained.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Price for the 2 GB SKU on Hardkernel's store as of 2026-04-26.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Price for the 4 GB SKU on Hardkernel's store as of 2026-04-26 (raised from the 2020 launch price of $50 due to DRAM costs).

available-regions.comment: Sold direct from Hardkernel (KR) plus AmeriDroid (US), Pollin/Antratek (EU), Botland (PL), and others.

typical-lead-time.comment: In stock at multiple resellers as of 2026-04-26, though Hardkernel reported tight supply through late 2024 / early 2025 before ramping production ~30%.

documentation-quality.comment: Hardkernel publishes a structured wiki with hardware reference, schematics PDF, application notes, and getting-started guides — comprehensive but not at the polish level of Raspberry Pi's docs.

community-size.comment: ODROID family has an established multi-decade community via the official forum and ODROID Magazine.

active-forum-discord.comment: Hardkernel runs the official ODROID forum, which sees daily activity including frequent vendor staff responses.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Hardkernel sells official cases, cooling fans, eMMC modules, RTC battery, USB Wi-Fi modules, and an RTC shield. Smaller third-party accessory market than Raspberry Pi but solid for an ODROID-class board.
